acceptance_targets: [ch04_dialogue_001] PASS if in revised 503-533 each of Yoon, Oh, and Choi has at least one line whose diction/rhythm is uniquely attributable without dialogue tags and at least one direct rebuttal shifts leverage; FAIL if lines remain cross-swappable.

[ch04_dialogue_002] PASS if revised Kwon dialogue in those spans limits repeated abstraction keywords to one use each and includes at least two concrete consequence statements tied to this production; FAIL if abstraction cluster still dominates.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if in revised 503-533 each of Yoon, Oh, and Choi has at least one line whose diction/rhythm is uniquely attributable without dialogue tags and at least one direct rebuttal shifts leverage; FAIL if lines remain cross-swappable.
findings.problem: Containment-call voices (Yoon, Oh, Choi) converge into similar formal compliance syntax, weakening idiolect separation and making pressure exchanges sound interchangeable.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:503-533, differentiate each speaker's register: Yoon as legal-risk precision, Oh as ideological mandate framing, Choi as talent-economics/brand control, with distinct rhythm and interruption habits.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised Kwon dialogue in those spans limits repeated abstraction keywords to one use each and includes at least two concrete consequence statements tied to this production; FAIL if abstraction cluster still dominates.
findings.problem: Kwon's diction over-reuses abstract control tokens (weather/signal/architecture/vector/corridor), reading like mechanical lexical-signature reuse rather than adaptive speech under changing pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Kwon's lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:118-128, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:336-342, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:590-596 so at least part of his pressure language turns concrete (specific legal triggers, rating penalties, personnel consequences) rather than repeating abstraction tokens.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on dialogue realism and voice separation: idiolect fidelity, leverage shifts, pressure cadence, anti-transcript behavior, natural colloquial texture (contractions/interruptions/slang when character-true), and pressure-true profanity usage per aesthetic risk policy.
revision_pass.label: dialogue/idiolect/cadence fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
